codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original beta-command-webhooks handoff/commit together with the follow-up fix commit. The original failure on stable command idempotency and retry-safe atomic note-command effects is addressed: command-triggered rerun/focus runs now derive deterministic keys from the delivery identifier, and note hook persistence plus command execution now share one transaction so failed attempts roll back cleanly and can be retried without losing side effects.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-integration skill still assumes integration fixes typically add new tests, but this rerun fix correctly closed the issue by tightening the transactional interface and reusing pre-existing regression coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-19T18:57:25.005Z for beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix reports followedProcedure=false with suggestedChanges noting that updating existing regression coverage was sufficient; the fix commit 8f7e52f only changes internal/hooks/handler.go while targeted tests TestCommandIdempotencyStableDeliveryKey, TestCommandRetryAfterPersistenceFailure, and TestCommandPersistenceAndExecutionAtomicity already existed in internal/hooks/handler_command_integration_test.go.

summary: Pass. I reviewed feature metadata, the prior failed review, both handoffs/transcript skeleton evidence, commits 29ae864468dfe743886317306885fd4242d72954 and 8f7e52f, the command and webhook implementation, and the targeted regression tests. The fix adequately resolves the prior blocking concerns: delivery-based idempotency keys prevent duplicate rerun/focus effects for replayed note deliveries, and transactional note-command handling prevents a persisted hook_event from suppressing a retried command after partial failure.
